desc:
You make your way through the increasingly dense vegetation and reach the foot
of the cliff. Its face is sheer, as it runs about a hundred yards straight up.
There is absolutely no way one could scale the cliff at this side. On its top
lies the even taller tower of the citadel. For a very brief moment you get the
sensation it is all falling down on you! No wonder the citizens of Fornost
change the subject whenever the old castle is brought up! South of here you can
spot a section of the city walls.
name: Ruined Park under the High Cliff

desc:
This pond lies just below the base of the cliff. A small, decorative stream of
water flows through a hole in the side of the cliff a dozen or so feet above the
surface, and gently plunges down into the pond.
name: Small Pond under the Cliff

desc:
You stand among some ruins, at the edge of what seems once to have been a
magnificent park. But that was obviously long ago, and centuries of free growth
have turned it into quite a wilderness. Westwards past this wilderness a high,
black cliff thrusts into the sky.
name: Overgrown Ruins

desc:
You are just inside the gates of the town, with a wild park to the west and an
irregular clump of buildings in the north and east. There seem to be no major
roads here except for the one you're standing on, The King's Road, which leads
north further into the city and south out through the main gates. To the east
lies a building with thick walls, but you can't see any entrance on this side.
name: By the Gate

desc:
You are standing here between two portals in a cobblestone passage under the
outer wall of the city of Fornost. The southern one is a portcullis, usually
raised in times of peace. The gate to the north is iron-clad and looks extremely
massive. Glancing up you notice machicolations through which hot oil or missiles
can be rained down upon an invading enemy. A bronze plaque is fixed on the wall.
name: Under the Wall, between the Gates

desc:
You enter a fairly large yard, most of its cobblestones covered with dirt. The
building that occupies the eastern and northern ends of the square is a
two-story, red painted barn. Two double doors allow you to enter in any of those
directions. Between the doors is a large stack of yellow hay. The northern
entrance has the words 'Gordun's Fine Animals' painted over it in big letters.
Now the building to the south has thick walls of stone, with very few and very
small windows, almost like an extension of the city's wall. You see soldiers
passing in and out of its doorway.
name: Animal's Court

desc:
Here you can turn over your mount to the care of the people at the stables.
Although the building itself doesn't seem to be in a very good condition, with
large parts of the ceiling and walls missing, you think your mount will do just
fine here. His needs are obviously not as high as yours. A small note sits on
one of the walls.
name: The Fornost Stables

desc:
You step out on the cranking wooden floor of this large, high barn. The big
doors swung open easily since several of the planks making up the walls are
missing. This makes the environment in here much the same as the one outside.
For all sorts of animals, this is a temporary home. Their many different scents
reach your nose and the combination is not a pleasant smell! There is a sign
placed on one of the walls.
name: Gordun's Animal Market

desc:
This shop is filled with fabric of different colours and varying materials. The
tailor sits on a stool in a corner, while his apprentices are busy measuring out
the customers. There is a note on one of the walls.
name: The Tailor's Shop

desc:
You walk among the trees and bushes, just below the high cliff harbouring the
old citadel. Although large parts of the city have been rebuilt, the Dunedain
rangers have yet to reach the castle. It sits high up there. Strong, dark and
silent. It suddenly makes you feel VERY small. You release the castle from your
thoughts as you turn your attention to the beauty of the surrounding wilderness
instead. The King's road can be seen a bit east, through the trees.
name: Old, Wild-grown Park

desc:
This broad road looks very old, and it is clear that it has been patched up
recently. This is also your general impression of the city and its walls. To the
south you can see the main gates. Just east is a small square. A park lies to
the west directly below the high cliff on which the old castle of Fornost is
situated.
name: The King's Road

desc:
As the street reaches the thick walls of Fornost's garrison, it makes a sharp
turn to the east. A large canopy shelters the corner. The wild street-trade you
encountered along this street becomes more specialized, as this seem to be the
place where jesters, musicians, and artists demonstrate their skills, in hope of
selling their services.
name: Jester's Corner

desc:
Walking along this street of trade and commerce, you get the feeling that after
a long struggle, good times are finally coming along for the inhabitants, with
the promise of prosperity just around the corner. Craftsmen's alley continues
east and west.
name: Craftsmen's Alley

desc:
This is where Fornost's magistrates gather, debate, and sometimes even come to a
decision! However they don't appear to be in session today, but there is a board
on which you can read other peoples opinions, and perhaps express some of your
own.
name: City Council's Meeting Room

desc:
Although this place is called market square, you cannot see any signs of that
particular activity going on here. Either the markets occur on special days, and
this isn't one of them, or maybe the name just stuck from the old days. Here on
the northern end of the square you can see the building which contain the
city-council's meeting place and the post office. A large red sign can be seen
hanging over the entrance to a house on the southern end of the square.
name: Market Square

desc:
Oh, what a display of shields and fine protective clothing! Shopping here is a
must for every true warrior and all other people who have to fight for their
living. Especially wonderful are the glittering chain mail clothes in bronze and
silver.
name: The King's Armoury

desc:
Judging from the state of the neighbouring buildings and streets this is one of
the finest, if not the finest, areas in the city. There are no traces of trash
or ruins, and while the buildings around the square might be old, they have all
been restored to mint-condition. Pleasant vegetation can be seen to the east,
and streets lead south and west. A massive rectangular pedestal bearing a bronze
plaque occupies the centre of the square.
name: Arnor Square

desc:
With the city wall at your back, you stand facing the south, contemplating the
architecture of the large building in front of you. A short but wide flight of
stairs leads up to its entrance, which lie past a row of massive columns. The
building is rectangular with windows and ornaments on the walls. You realise you
are standing outside the famous old Library.
name: Guild Street

desc:
You note yet another sign of the neighbourhoods prosperity; this street is the
only one you've seen so far where the cobblestones are clean and free of the
usual mud, dust or trash which can be seen everywhere else.
name: Guild Street

desc:
Guild Street runs east and west with the city wall on its northern side and a
row of important-looking houses on its southern. This seem to be the place where
all the various guild of the area are situated: the cooper, potter, mason and
shoemaker guilds can be seen further down the street. Most notable though, the
warrior-guild is just south of here.
name: Guild Street

desc:
This is the place where martial skills are learned and perfected. At the walls
hang different kinds of weapons, mostly slashing, so you can practice all the
associated fighting techniques. The teacher here is said to be a specialist of
the parry skill, so he might show some new tricks even to experienced fighters.
From here you can go back north to Guild Street. A staircase leads up to a room
that seems suitable for a little break after the training or perhaps a chat with
some fellow warriors.
name: The Guild of Warriors

desc:
You have arrived at the gateway which takes you through the wall to the old not
yet reconstructed part of this ancient town. This wall was raised to shield the
citizens of new Fornost from the shadows that still reign among the ruins of the
city's western parts and the old castle. Every now and then soldiers open the
gate to allow for rangers to pass through. Some of the returning ones are
bleeding and have obviously been fighting quite recently. The words 'ENTER AT
YOUR OWN RISK' are written in large letters on the gate.
name: Gate in the Palisade

desc:
You enter a small cabin which serves as shelter for the soldiers posted at the
gate. The only furniture is a table and some chairs.
name: Guardhouse

desc:
You have come into the middle of the slums of Fornost. What a shabbiness! Bitter
faces are looking out of half dilapidated houses, children are playing in the
dirty gutters. You can see beggars here, starving. Give them a few coppers, they
need it to survive. To the south you can see the poorhouse. A palisade runs from
the northern city wall to the base of the cliff which lie a bit south, cutting
the city in two parts.
name: The King's Road by the Palisade

desc:
This is the place where the poor people of Fornost can get a blanket and a hard
plank bed for the night. It's very depressing here, and you hope you won't end
up in a place like this. From time to time a charitable woman comes to drop off
some old breadcrumbs.
name: The Poorhouse

desc:
This is a small, shabby place. The room is dimly lit and the customers all seem
very mysterious, keeping low profiles at their tables. This also goes for the
owner of the place. He glares suspiciously at you as you enter, and all
conversation is halted. Then, as your eyes meet his, he quickly returns to
cleaning some cups. Not a very hospitable place it would seem!
name: Olly's Tavern

desc:
The road runs through this part of the city, which seem more ruined than
rebuilt. At some places evidence of on-going construction is visible; scaffolds
and strange machinery, shovels and other tools can be seen. To the west, the
road turns again as it reaches a wooden wall.
name: Ruined King's Road

desc:
This part of the King's Road is very solitary and in spite of the low growl of
Fornost all around, very silent. Small sounds carry far. The domestic sounds
present in houses on the east side of the street are clearly audible and the
echoing footsteps of an approaching person precede him by a long time.
name: Ruined King's Road

desc:
Along the street you see the typical houses of Fornost, high but very narrow
stone buildings, with roofs either flat, or slanted ones made of dried grass.
There were probably wooden buildings once, but they have failed to stand the
test of time and where they once stood there are now only hollow gaps.
name: The King's Road

desc:
This is the junction of the King's Road, which runs north to south, and the
Short Street that leads eastward into the heart of Fornost where busy merchants
and shoppers can be heard going about their business. Beyond that lies the Crown
of Arnor Inn, where travellers and citizens alike tend to gather. Though the
people of the city have a rough and ready look about them, for the most part the
city is quite clean and well-maintained.
name: Junction on the King's Road

desc:
The road continues north and south. West, an overgrown park spreads out beneath
a high cliff. East of the road lies the houses of the common Fornost citizen, in
a somewhat shabby neighbourhood.
name: The King's Road

desc:
This street is exactly what its name says, short. It runs from the Kings Road in
the west to Market Square at the centre of the city. Along the road on both
sides narrow, tall houses can be seen. To the north, a large old building
dominates the view because of a regal look that gives it an almost holy
appearance.
name: Short Street

desc:
You are standing in the reception area of the thermal baths. The air is warm and
moist, carrying with it the scents of all sorts of perfumes and oils. A mosaic
picture of Ulmo, lord of the waters, covers the floor.
name: Entrance to the Thermal Baths

desc:
Aaaaahhhh! You slide into the warm water of this large, shallow pool and
suddenly feel totally relaxed! The water comes from a natural warm spring. It
has a light-yellowish colour, and smells slightly of sulphur. The warm water and
the moist air make you feel very sleepy ...
name: In the Pool

desc:
As you enter the room, making some noise, you get angry looks from the scholars
in here. They are all very silent with conversations held in whispering voices.
Most of the people in the room are sitting at special writing-tables, copying
the works of long dead masters.
name: The Writing Room

desc:
You have entered the legendary Great Library of Fornost, which before its ruin
nine hundred years ago held the largest collection of books and scrolls in this
part of the world. Nowadays, it is not nearly as impressive, although once again
the library has managed to gain a good reputation among human scholars. A
massive staircase leads up to the collection itself.
name: The Entrance of the Library

desc:
This small passage is so narrow you can barely bring a horse through it. It
connects two squares; Market in the south and Arnor just north. On either side
of the alley, tall, gaunt houses shoot up against the sky, with time-stained
fronts and windows that have grown dim with age.
name: Narrow Alley

desc:
This is the north-western corner of the square, where a very narrow alley leads
north, away from the crowds, into quieter parts of the city. The city wall can
be seen east of the square, giving a sudden and unwelcome reminder of the city's
violent past. The entrance to 'The Traveller's Shop' is to the west.
name: Western End of Market Square

desc:
This is the centre of the new, rebuilt Fornost. A few short years ago this place
was abandoned and deserted like the rest of this ancient city, but it has
already developed a thriving trade. The inhabitants are all doing well it seems,
and hoping to do better still. Trays of fish and fruit indicate that the shop
west of here is a grocery of some sort. A street leading south plunges a hole in
the otherwise massive front of stores and houses that border the square.
name: Western End of Market Square

desc:
You walk in a narrow alley, bordered with two and three story houses, tightly
packed together. The houses' ground floors are often used as shops or working
areas for the craftsmen this street is so suitably named after, while the
families live on the upper floors.
name: Craftsmen's Alley

desc:
You have ended up in the chaotic circus of street-commerce. The loud voices of
fishmongers, butchers, blacksmiths, carpenters, or just anybody with something
to sell, makes normal conversation practically impossible. You try and quicken
your pace, but it is useless; the street is just too crowded. You can escape the
crowd west, into a small tailor's shop.
name: Craftsmen's Alley

desc:
This short street runs in an east-west fashion. The town's gates lie to the west
while the Market Square and the Crown of Arnor Inn can be found to the east, on
either side of the street, houses line the road. Rows of laundry stretch between
the houses and in some cases over the road, the larger items catching the wind
and flapping noisily.
name: Short Street

desc:
This is a large, spacious shop with enough room for all sorts of goods. You see
shelves full of food, partly ordinary, partly exotic, imported from foreign
lands and grown and produced by other races. You see fruits and vegetables,
spices and herbs. A small sign hangs over the counter.
name: The Grocery

desc:
In this shop you can buy all that you might need on your travels or adventures.
On the walls and shelves you see lanterns, sacks, ropes, bags, shovels and all
kinds of gadgets and stuff that could prove to be useful out there on the roads
or in the wilderness. There is a sign on the counter.
name: The Traveller's Shop

desc:
As you enter this large rectangular room, you remember hearing good words about
'The Crown' on your travels, and the first impressions are not disappointing.
The room extends further east, where food and drinks are being served. In the
middle of the room's southern wall a fire is sparkling on a hearth. A flight of
stairs leads up to the customers' private chambers. It is clear however that the
arrangements would be made down here, should you want to rent a room.
name: The Crown of Arnor Inn

desc:
Here, people can learn much to help them survive the harsh wilderness. Skills
such as tracking, riding and other useful skills are taught by experienced
Dunadan rangers, who some consider to be the most knowledgeable in the ways of
the wild in all of Arda.
name: Ranger's Guild

desc:
The people come here to watch and perhaps put some money on fighting animals.
Often roosters, but not always. It could be a bear fighting some dogs, or maybe
a captured orc, weakened by the sun. The winner lives to fight again another
day. The loser of course isn't that lucky.
name: Cockfighting Grounds

desc:
Having left the civilised part of Fornost, you now stand in front of something
that looks more like a sparse forest, than a ruined city. Time has long since
caught up with the old houses, and grass and bushes cover them. However, the
road you are standing on still seems to be traversable westwards, among the
ruins.
name: West of the Wooden Gate

desc:
Just to the east, the wooden palisade rises, to protect the inhabitants of
Fornost from whatever lives on this side. To the north, you see the remains of
the King's Road that once ran from the main gates to the castle that lies on the
cliff that towers above you to the west and the south.
name: Ruins at the Wooden Wall

desc:
Here, just behind the wooden wall, that the citizens of "new" Fornost put up,
the scene is quite different. Remains of old houses lie as piles of stone blocks
on the ground, overgrown by grass and small trees. Nothing remains to remind you
of the beautiful city that once stood here, except the old castle, that still
stands on a high hill to the south-west. To the north, the old city wall still
stands, protecting the world outside from the dangers that lie within.
name: Ruins at the Wooden Wall

desc:
Moss clad stones are the only sign of this part of Arthedain's old capital. No
man has lived here for over nine hundred years, and only small animals run
around between the rocks these days.
name: Grassy Ruins

desc:
There is certainly nothing royal about this road any more. The square white
stones that once formed an easily travelled road, slowly climbing up to the
castle above, now are cracked and crooked.
name: Ruined King's Road

desc:
Grass and bushes cover the ruins of Fornost Erain. Looking around you, you
notice the ruined road to the south, the city wall to the north, and nothing but
more ruins in the other directions. Further south, you see the dark castle,
overlooking the once great city. A large tree stands here between the rocks.
name: Overgrown Ruins

desc:
To the north you see the only real evidence of the old city that once stood
here. It is a part of the outer walls of Fornost, that has survived the long
years since the invasion of the Witch-king. To the south, there is a small,
withered road, and beyond it, you see the ominous Royal Castle of Fornost.
name: Ruins of Fornost

desc:
The road still runs straight from the east to the west, slowly climbing its way
up on the hill that the western part of Fornost was once built upon. Grass,
bushes and small trees stand everywhere around you in the ruined houses.
name: Ruined King's Road

desc:
It seems that you should be careful if you wish to travel in this area, for
stones and rubble litter the ground, half-hidden among the grass and the tall
weeds, ready to trip you. These broken fragments of larger stones are all that
remains of the old city of Fornost, and now this place is home only to the
unseen creatures who can be faintly heard, scurrying through the undergrowth.
name: Among the Ruins

desc:
Once, houses bordered this road, but they all lie in ruins these days. The road
is sort of intact though, and can take you north or west from here. Looking
south, you see the old castle sitting silently on the hill, its high towers
stretching high into the sky.
name: Ruined King's Road

desc:
The road bends here. To the east, it leads to the safety of the civilised parts
of Fornost, and to the south, it slowly climbs around the hill from which the
old castle looks down on the ruins that surround you.
name: Ruined King's Road

desc:
The high wall of Fornost still stands to the north, preventing you from any
attempt to go that way. To the south you see an old road leading towards the
large hill with the abandoned castle on top. Looking west, you see what might be
an old house still standing against the wall.
name: At the Old City Wall

desc:
This is the place for thieves to learn their evil abilities. You see some
assassins practising their skills, and in a few weeks they will be ready to go
out and use their knowledge on the streets.
name: The Guild of the Black Dagger

desc:
You have found a secret room beneath the old ruin. There is very little light in
here and you sense people hiding in the deep shadows around you. A black dagger
with a neatly carved hilt is fastened to the door that leads east of here. Few
men have seen this place and lived to tell about it.
name: Entrance to the Black Dagger Guild

desc:
This must be one of the very few houses that have lasted since the city was
virtually destroyed. Its northern wall is actually the old city wall, and you
can leave the house through the empty doorway to the east. The floor is covered
with dirt and grass, and as you look up, you see that the entire ceiling is
gone.
name: In the Ruined House

desc:
All around you, you see grass-covered piles of stones, but to the north of you
stands what appears to be a fairly intact house. To the south you can see the
ruined remains of the King's Road.
name: Among the Ruins

desc:
The road runs east and west here. Looking south, you can see a pile of many
stones - obviously the remnants of a larger house. The remains of a wall
prevents you from going very far in that direction. A small stone catches your
eye.
name: Ruined King's Road

desc:
This small height once was a place of death. The Dunedain never penalized anyone
with death, but orcs hung people in large numbers - a far more merciful fate
than the one that could await you at their torturers in the dungeons of the old,
ruined castle that towers south of here.
name: Gallows Hill

desc:
This street once led to the gallows hill just east of here. Now, no one has used
it for over eight hundred years, and it lies in ruins. The cobblestones that
used to be perfectly lined up now lie in a mess, and most of them are overgrown
by thick, brownish grass.
name: Old Gallows Street

desc:
Carefully looking around for traces of the civilisation that once stood here,
you notice that the once great King's Road crosses another decayed road which
leads from an old square to the north towards a small hill just south of here.
name: Ruined King's Road

desc:
Here, in the middle of what seems to have been a small square, you see a stone
circle, that once was a well. Now, there is nothing left but a dark hole in the
ground. To the west, you see some houses that seem to have withstood the test of
time, with their walls still standing.
name: At the Old Fountain

desc:
There are many large white stones littering the area, obviously the common
building material of that era. However, the layout of the stones bears no
resemblance to the buildings of the great city that once stood here. Everything
that consisted of wood must have rotted away centuries ago, leaving nothing but
a hostile, scary sea of grass covered stones.
name: Overgrown Ruins

desc:
Just to the north of here the city wall stretches some thirty feet into the air
above you. The city that once lay within seems to be far away though, since the
only thing you can spot around you are small birches and a few bushes.
name: At the Wall

desc:
This rather small house must once have been the home of a medium sized family.
The doors to the east and south have long since rotted, but the gaps have
allowed animals to run in and out rather freely, which, from the faeces that lie
all about you, they obviously have done.
name: In an Overgrown House

desc:
You have now reached the northwestern corner of the once mighty city. The high
city wall turns from the east to the south here and to the east you see the back
of a small house. The breach in the wall that you passed on your way here is
clearly visible to the south.
name: At the Corner of the City Wall

desc:
In the year of 1974, when the foul orcs of Angmar attacked Fornost, they managed
to break down the city wall at this very point. Looking through the wide hole,
you see a green forest stretching in all directions. A few houses with their
walls still standing can be seen to the east, but most of the city that once lay
here seems to be gone.
name: At the Breach in the Wall

desc:
A small grove of birches has taken root here in the ruins of the city. An
occasional rock sticks up between the roots, but not much else is left to see
here.
name: Among the Trees

desc:
Not much is left to see of the road that once led north from the King's Road
towards the city wall. Looking west, you see the wall of a crooked house, with
its empty windows gaping at the surroundings. To the south you can see the
remains of the once busy Kings Road.
name: A Decaying Road

desc:
The wide road that was once the King's Road crosses a smaller street that leads
from the south to the north. The King's Road used to be over forty yards wide
and easily travelled on the perfectly fitting white stones from the White Downs.
Now, war and nature has slowly broken it down, and only a few broken stones peek
out from the undergrowth reminding you of its former beauty.
name: Ruined King's Road

desc:
A small, once paved, street leads north and south. Passages to the east and west
are blocked by the crumbled walls of old houses. To the north, you see the
ruined King's Road, and to the south there is the remains of an old house.
name: The Small Street

desc:
From the layout of this ruined building, you quickly decide that it must once
have been one of the stables of Fornost. Arthedain never really used cavalry,
but horses were widely used for transportation, and from the adorned walls, you
see that the Dunedain cared a lot about their mounts.
name: Inside the Old Stables

desc:
The road is getting rather steep, making motion rather slow. Just south of here,
the road meets another, smaller road in a T-junction. To the east and west,
steep slopes offer no grip for climbing.
name: Ruined King's Road

desc:
Looking around you, you see the remnants of a house that is now completely
destroyed. Crumbled remnants of the buildings walls lie all about you, reminding
you of how violent the war must have been.
name: Inside the Fallen Walls

desc:
You can still see the remainder of some neat flower beds in this small backyard.
It surely must have been the pride and joy of some skilled housewife several
hundred years ago, before the orcs came here and mutilated everything.
name: At the Small Backyard

desc:
The roof of this small building is since long gone, lying rotted to dust
somewhere on the floor probably. To the east is the large road that once was the
King's Road, and to the south you see something that looks like a small
backyard, perhaps used for herbs once long ago.
name: In the Roofless House

desc:
The road turns here in a straight ninety degrees angle. To the south, it slowly
begins to climb the hill towards the ruined castle, and to the east it can take
you back towards the inhabited parts of Fornost Erain - the northern fortress of
the kings.
name: Ruined King's Road

desc:
The wide road starts to narrow as it begins to wind its way towards the top of
the hill with the castle on top. Thick tufts of grass have emerged between the
crooked paving-stones that still lie here covered in moss and lichen.
name: Ruined King's Road

desc:
The road that led up here is nothing but a small path now. It turns here from
the east to the south, where it climbs around the hill. Just north of here you
see the crumbled walls of a house, destroyed ages ago.
name: Bend in the Path

desc:
Here, the wide road meets a smaller street that leads east on a wide ledge
around the hill that lies to the south east. To the west, the road leads up on
the hill to the gates of the royal castle.
name: Ruined King's Road

desc:
This old street leads halfway up the castle hill that rises to the south. You
cannot find any spot to climb the steep cliff between you and the castle and not
far to the north is the edge of a steep cliff which would surely mean certain
death for any climbers.
name: The Old Street

desc:
The small street ends here with a large house to the south. It has withstood
wind and rain, and still stands as a memory of the beauty once seen throughout
the city. The street leads halfway up the hill on which the Royal Castle still
stands, sheer drops to the north and the east clearly indicate that there is no
way to proceed.
name: End of the Old Street

desc:
As you enter the old building, you can't help being impressed by the skill of
the Dunedain masons that once built it. Beautiful ornaments surround the now
empty door posts and a splendid mosaic on the south wall, now covered with dust,
shows a picture of what must have been the city of Fornost at the peak of its
beauty.
name: Inside the Old House

desc:
Once these barracks must have offered lodging for hundreds of soldiers. However,
there is very little left of this old building - most probably it was virtually
destroyed when the thousands of orcs from Angmar poured in over the city.
name: Ruined Barracks

desc:
The path quickly climbs its way south and up, towards the outer gates of the
castle that lies south east of here. At this point, it passes the ruins of a
large house to the east, and to the west, you see a small path sloping down
towards the foot of the hill.
name: Sloping Path to the Old Castle

desc:
The small path you are standing on zigzags its way past boulders and trees,
taking you either north to some kind of statue at the base of the hill or east
to the path leading up to the castle.
name: Winding Path up the Hill

desc:
A large statue stands here in memory of the fallen elves and humans that lie
buried behind the iron gate to the west. Looking through the bars of the gate,
you see a large tree-covered burial mound surrounded by a soft mist. To the
south, a small path winds its way up the foot of the hill.
name: Entrance to the Burial Grounds

desc:
Many dark, silent, ancient oak trees stand on guard over the graves of the
valiant elves of the Grey Havens and men of Gondor and Arthedain. The many
gravestones tell of their valiant deeds and their heroic contribution to the
liberation of Fornost. This place is certainly no place for a living man to
linger.
name: The Burial Mound

desc:
This is the top of the gloomy burial mound that contains the bodies of the men
and elves that died trying to drive the orcs out of the city. Strange shadows
mixed with a light mist drift between the black oaks that loom over the dead.
name: Top of the Burial Mound

desc:
This room has been sealed off since the year of the great war. The air is
anything but pleasant to breathe and you hold your nose in disgust as you slowly
enter the dark room. A sudden sound from the darkness catches your attention and
you quickly try to make out what caused it before something nasty jumps out at
you...
name: The Hidden Room

desc:
A small stairway leads up from this small, and empty basement to the slab of
stone that has kept visitors out since the old days of the war. The dirt walls
have begun to collapse, and you don't feel very safe in here as suddenly you
hear even more dirt falling down from the eastern wall.
name: The Empty Basement

desc:
You are standing in a green forest growing at the foot of a high wall to the
east. The wall is several paces high, and once contained the prospering city of
Fornost Erain. Looking between the high trees, you see that there is a wide
breach in the wall at this very spot, allowing anyone, who has the nerves it
takes, to enter the abandoned city.
name: Forest outside the Breach in the Wall

desc:
You are standing on the remnants of the road that leads to the castle on the
hill above, overlooking the ruined city behind you. To the east, there is a
small path slowly climbing upwards, and you have to walk carefully not to trip
over the old, crooked cobblestones.
name: Sloping Path to the Old Castle

desc:
The path slowly takes you up the hill. Once, this was a road paved with cut
stones from the White Downs far in the west of Arthedain. It has, however, not
been in use since the big war with Angmar, when the city below you was ruined,
leaving nothing but crumbled walls and the surprisingly intact castle that looms
above you to the east.
name: Sloping Path to the Old Castle

desc:
Finally, you have reached the end of the withered path. You are on a small
drawbridge that can take you into the black gap where the gates of the castle
once were. Looking downhill, to the west, you can only see traces of the road
that used to lead up here. Looking over the edge of the bridge, you see the dike
which was once used to stop the ferocious hordes of orcs that plundered Fornost
a millennium ago.
name: On the Castle's Drawbridge

desc:
This room obviously once contained the sleeping-chambers of the gate guards.
Looking around, you can see old pieces of rotten wood that were once the
furnishings in this dark cellar. An old stone stairway leads to the floor above.
name: The Guards' Quarters

desc:
Jumping down from the drawbridge, you find yourself stuck in the steep dike that
was once used to prevent any invasion attempts against the royal castle that
towers above you. Unfortunately, you can't seem to find any way up from here.
name: Below the Drawbridge

desc:
Looking up, you see a sturdy stone arch holding the battlements above on its
shoulders. Examining the wall to the west closer, you see that the great hinges
that once held the gates are nothing but rust, and the gates themselves are
nothing but dust lying on the ground. You can either enter the outer yard of the
castle to the east, go back on the drawbridge or go through the black hole that
once was a door to the north.
name: Inside the Gates

desc:
The road follows the high wall to the south. It can take you either uphill, to
the east, where the citadel lies, or downhill to the silent ruins of Fornost
below. High grass grows everywhere, making it obvious that no man has travelled
here in a very long time.
name: Road inside the Walls

desc:
The road leaves the gates of the outer wall to the west, and slowly climbs the
small slope that will take you to the main building of the castle in the
north-east. South of here, the high battlements rises above you, overlooking the
surroundings.
name: Road inside the Walls

desc:
Looking around you, you see the high outer wall to the east, and a large stone
house to the west, its roof since long gone, but the walls still look intact.
Behind it, you see the high towers of the citadel rising high into the air.
name: Road inside the Walls

desc:
This is the final part of the old road that leads from the outer gates to the
citadel that lies west of here, on the other side of a small square. The road
continues south, taking you downhill, past the remains of a rather large stone
building.
name: Road inside the Walls

desc:
You stand in a small cavern, with water up to your waist. A stream of water is
coming down from above. As you investigate this further, you realise that the
walls are too slippery and steep to climb, and perhaps it would be best to leave
it at that.
name: In a Small Pool

desc:
You can hear, or rather feel, the sounds of the city. It all seems far away as
you are now walking through this low, moist tunnel with water to your ankles.
name: A Low, Damp Tunnel

desc:
The small but steady stream of water at your feet flows through an old, rusted
grating at the eastern end of the tunnel. Outside the grating, you can see
trees, and a couple of feet down, a small pond.
name: A Low, Damp Tunnel

desc:
You are now standing just north of the point where the path starts turning east,
around the hill. You can see the silhouette of the castle here as you turn your
gaze to the east, where it lies silently on its hill.
name: Sloping Path to the Old Castle

desc:
Various pieces of machinery, fallen into disrepair from lack of care, lie in one
part of the room, next to spare pots of oil for the defence of the Fornost
gates. An empty rack at one time may have held spare weapons for the soldiers,
or a last-ditch defence should the evil of Angmar strike the city again. This
place has been recently maintained however, and a heavy black curtain stretches
across the top of the stairs leading up. A few open barrels store reserves of
drinking water for the city.
name: A Holy Chapel

desc:
This tower room is wide with curving walls, which vault upwards into a larger
ceiling, from which hangs suspended a sparkling crystal chandelier, decked with
scented candles, which gives the room a rather relaxed and peaceful atmosphere.
Dim light is supplied from the overhead candles and the fireplace, which still
has a few logs that remain in it, ready to warm the interior against a harsh
winter. The floor is covered with rich plush carpeting, and the walls are hung
with tapestries. A large bookcase stands here, filled with ancient tomes. A
luxurious bed stands in the corner, covered with furs and silks.
name: A Dimly-lit Sanctuary

desc:
You are standing on the top of this old, crumbling tower. Like everything else
in Fornost, it has been heavily patched, so you proceed with caution as you lean
on the battlement and adore the splendid view of Fornost and its outskirts.
Thick green forests lie to the west, as well as the ruined parts of the city,
that have fallen into decay. A brightly-coloured banner hangs from the ramparts,
flapping in the wind.
name: Tower at the Gate

desc:
You have entered a large hall on the upper floor of the library, which contain
the collection. The room has the obvious furniture: Shelves, shelves and more
shelves, packed with books and scrolls. The hall extends further west.
name: Among the Bookshelves

desc:
The bookcases here are not quite as packed as the ones in the eastern end of the
hall, allowing space for the collection to grow. You can see a couple of
comfortable chairs arranged in a circle around a table. There are also some
ladders, which are needed to get books off the higher shelves.
name: Among the Bookshelves

desc:
This is one of the most ruined and desolate places you have seen in Fornost. It
looks as if it has once been a large house of entertainment, but the roof has
fallen in at many places, and the stairs are steep, rugged and broken. There is
a huge fireplace in the room and the chimney is blackened with smoke; but no
warm blaze lights it up now. The white feathery dust of burnt wood is still
strewn over the hearth, but the stove is cold, and all is dark and gloomy.
name: Abandoned Building

desc:
Strangely shaped bottles, flasks and containers fill the large workbench in the
centre of the room. Some contain boiling liquids in all different colours,
others are broken or empty. The room is filled with a smoke which, apart from
making you dizzy, also makes it hard to see anything more than a few feet away.
name: The Alchemist's Workroom

desc:
Shelves upon shelves adorn the walls of this library. Delicate oil lamps give
the room a bright glow. There are books and scrolls scattered all over the
house--on shelves, on tables, on the floor, even on most of the chairs. Some of
the books seem to be quivering and vibrating slightly on their own, as though
the magic contained within is trying to escape. A silver tea service has been
left in the exact centre of a small occasional table, ready for its owner to
take a rest from his labours. In one corner of the room, that gets little light,
there is a mahogany liquor cabinet securely locked by ensorcellment and a
devious mechanical catch. From time to, visitors to this place get a feeling
that something is guarding the books here and it would be unwise to one of them
without permission.
name: Feera's Private Library

desc:
Leaving the loud bustle of the city, you have entered a hallway covered in old
paint. It quietly leads to the north where it meets up with a brightly lit
kitchen.
name: A Long Hallway

desc:
From the hallway south, you enter a large room filled with strange utensils and
the smell of fresh baked bread. A small fire burns at one end of the room,
providing light and warmth to those who visit. On the far end you see a broad
oak door providing passage to the north.
name: A Kitchen

desc:
This is a large chamber, decorated with artefacts, paintings and art work from
times long past. To the side of the room lies a small table with two chairs
neatly tucked beside it. A large bed lies in the centre of the room, laced with
white silk sheets and satin pillows. The air itself feels warm and relaxing.
name: Night's Bedchamber

desc:
The room is mostly unfurnished, allowing one to easily move around and train. In
the centre of it lies a large straw mat where soldiers can practice their
fighting arts. A large shield hangs on the wall to the north, encrusted with the
symbol of the Rohirrim.
name: Training Room

desc:
A small simple bed lies in the corner of the room, along with several chests
which bear personal belongings inside. A large window is set on the east wall,
allowing you to view the citizens of Fornost as they go about their daily
duties.
name: Graug's Bedroom

desc:
You enter a small space, filled with the smell of metal and rust. To the side of
the room there are many pegs and racks supporting the weight of many fine suits
of armour and weapons. Most are shiny and new, although some show the wear of
the glory of battle.
name: Graug's Armoury

desc:
You find a warm room with many tables and chairs huddled around a fire at the
north side of the room you see a large granite fireplace, it warms the room
allowing you to feel at ease.
name: A Comfortable Living Room

desc:
You enter a simple bedroom, with a large oak bed at its centre. The room is
circular in nature and is kept very neat. Two bookshelves are set against the
walls, filled with books of the arcane arts.
name: Tumeric's Bedroom

desc:
As you enter this room, the noise of Fornost seems to fade into dead silence,
you see four straw mats laid along the floor. Obviously this is a place where
you can escape the world and be at peace.
name: Meditation Chamber

desc:
A strong aroma of meat, ale, and sweat fill this cosy tavern. With no natural
light source, the dark almost stifling atmosphere might be relaxing to
adventurers who have experienced a hard days work. Loud snoring and some other
muffled noises can be heard from the sleeping area.
name: A Savoury Tavern

desc:
The road makes yet another sharp turn, towards a passage through the wooden wall
which lies just west now. An old crumbling building lies north of the road, its
broken shutters slamming against the walls every now and then, reminding you
that all things must eventually come to an end.
name: The King's Road by the Palisade

desc:
Named for the eleventh Chieftain of the Dunedain, this road connects the ancient
gardens with the King's Road. The cobblestones are well worn, as much of the
city's traffic, warriors, adventurers and merchants, pass it on their way to the
southern or western gates of Fornost.
name: Arassuil's Way

desc:
Stepping into these ancient gardens, it might seem as if it is hundreds of miles
away from the city. In contrast to the noisy cobblestone streets, this greenery
exudes peace and calm. The clumps of flowers are partly overgrown, but their
shape is always neat. Flower bushes line the dirt paths that crisscross the
garden. To the south and west start cobblestone streets.
name: Ancient Gardens

desc:
Old ruins of a house stand in this rocky lot. The base still seems to be intact,
its condition most likely allowing another rebuild for anyone's need. At one
corner of the ruins, wild berry bushes grow in abundance. Looking down, one can
see a door made of stone leading down into the rock.
name: Old Ruins

desc:
The cobblestone road ends here, at the site of an old garden. To the north, a
small fence lines an entrance of a house which probably belongs to one of
Fornost's many citizens. A large pile of various pieces of furniture draws
attention just south of the road.
name: Garden Road

desc:
Off the busy Guild Street, the noise of the city seems to decrease. Small flower
gardens line the sides of the cobblestone road, looking well-tended and taken
care of all year round. The street continues westwards, moving closer to the
private houses of the citizens of Fornost.
name: Garden Road

desc:
Strolling along, you can't help to notice the attitude of people you encounter;
Scholars and wealthy merchants in expensive clothing, with clerks and servants
in hot pursuit, all seem to regard your presence here as a bit unfortunate. You
are something that doesn't quite belong here, but a necessary evil perhaps.
name: Guild Street

desc:
You have entered a crowded building, in which some kind of auction is taking
place. It is here that merchants, local or from far away places, gather in order
to trade, buy or sell their many different wares.
name: The House of Merchants

desc:
Building materials are piled up all over this place. Shops are to the left and
right though they look all unfinished. There are no workers here and everything
is covered with a thick layer of dust. It seems that there wasn't anyone working
on the shops for a long time. A portal is to the west leading to an overgrown
garden. A sign is nailed to the portal.
name: Arcade of Shops

desc:
A large overgrown garden in front of a marble palace to the south. It maybe once
was bright and pleasant but now it looks like no one has entered or cared for it
for many long years. A small path is leading towards the palace. It is covered
with a few washed out footprints.
name: Overgrown Garden

desc:
The inside of this palace also looks unfinished. A stairway is leading up and in
the east is the kitchen. The west side isn't finished yet. The main rooms are
further south. There is only a thin layer of dust covering the furniture. It
looks like someone is taking care of this place from time to time.
name: Marble Palace

desc:
Here warriors are among themselves so they can relax, heal their wounds and chat
about new weapons and new protective clothing. A bulletin board is mounted here,
its purpose is to spread such information. The room looks comfortable, with soft
chairs along the walls, and colourful carpets.
name: Warrior's Relaxation Room

desc:
This is a tall rectangular bedchamber. During the day, thin streams of light
enter the room through several grilled windows set high up the wall, almost to
the ceiling. A large four-poster bed, draped in embroidered material, stands
against the western wall. Beside it is a small oak cabinet, which looks to be
filled with personal artefacts. Against the eastern wall is a legendary sword
set into a magically glowing stone, raised atop a short marble dais. The
southern side of the room contains a series of iron weapons, armour and racks of
the finest equipment in the known world. An oak and mahogany-crafted doorway
leads northward to a warrior's common room.
name: A Warmaster's Bedchamber

desc:
Isolated from the noise and the crowd of the city, this room looks perfectly
maintained and well arranged by its owner. There are racks hanging on the walls
containing trophies taken from the battles fought by Aquiles, the legend from
Eriador, who keeps this home for resting and meditating from his deeds on the
battlefield. In the corner a couple plants and flowers decorate the room making
it a more comfortable place. The floor is entirely covered with fine carpets.
Over the front window hangs a soft curtain which protects the room from any
watchful eyes outside.
name: Inner Sanctum of Aquiles

desc:
All around there are weapons and shields adorning the walls. Many paintings
depict scenes of epic battles. In the far corner there is what appears to be a
small collection of digusting scalps apparently torn from those that have fallen
at the master of this dwelling's hand. This home obviously belongs to some great
and merciless warrior who might not be pleased to find strangers inside his
home.
name: Avid's Residence

desc:
Large pieces of disused or no longer wanted furniture have been piled up here.
Some wooden slates have started to rot so that grass and moss are spreading
their roots over them. Despite the first impression being quite ragged, a person
with few resources but good handicraft skills could find things to fix a place
up easily enough.
name: Pile of Rubble

desc:
This is a very nice garden with lots of flowers all arond. There seems to be
almost every kind of flowers you can ever imagine in here. Utgbald who lives
here must have some strange fixation about flowers that not many people know
about. North of here is Utgbalds living room, and to the south is the
magnificent Arnor Square.
name: A Nice Garden

desc:
This room probably once contained the gate guard of the castle. A hole in the
wall to the south, once filled by a door, takes you back towards the gates. In
one corner of the room, a stone stair leads down into the darkness of the
basement, and up in a small tower above.
name: The Room of the Gate Guards

desc:
This small tower overlooks the outer gates of the castle, and the drawbridge
below it. The battlements once continued both north and south, but to the north
they seem to be anything but safe to walk. A small stair leads down to the old
guard room.
name: In the Small Tower

desc:
You have reached the battlements just above the drawbridge. To the west, you see
a small, overgrown path winding slowly up the slope towards the citadel on the
small hill to the north east. You can continue your stroll on the battlements to
the east, although they don't look very inviting.
name: On the Battlements

desc:
The battlements are indeed in a poor state of repair here, and the narrow
walkway has crumbled almost completely away in some places, so that progress to
the east is quite impossible from here. The only choice is to head west, where
the walls are in a better condition.
name: Ruined Battlements

desc:
This looks to be the simple home of an old dwarf. There are several weapons
hanging on racks upon the walls that looked to have been acquired on the owner's
travels. In the far right corner there is a fireplace. When lit the flames
provide a light and warm the room. The bed sits to the left of the room and near
the door two beer barrels have been set.
name: Staub's Home

desc:
This room is smaller than the large hall to the north and lined with shelves dug
into the walls. The air is filled with the smoke of pipes from recent
celebrations and leftover plates from a grand feast are deposited here and
there. There is a large table here for visitors to use, as well as a few beds
for the weary friends of Khazdul.
name: Khazdul's Guest Room

desc:
Dark shelves made from hard, polished beech line the walls of this musty and
very much cluttered room. A rough, wooden desk divides the room into two
partitions, one for the customers and one for the shopkeeper. A single,
spluttering oil lamp hangs down from the ceiling, providing some scant lighting
to this windowless room. In the corners, huge rolls of tarred rope serve as
chairs for idle customers to sit on, and crates full of dubious-looking
merchandise have been haphazardy placed all over the creaky, unswept floor.
name: Cluttered Back Room

desc:
Tall granite cliffs to the west and south dominate the skyline of the flat land
below. A scattering of short shrubs decorates the short, maintained grass, and a
lightly-defined path runs north through it. On the eastern end of the field, a
small pool of clear water bubbles out of the ground. The stream it forms flows
west, amidst the grass, rocks, and shrubs.
name: Field under Grey Peaks

desc:
Sheer rock faces of smooth granite rise high to the north and east. The tree
cover is very thin in this field, and only a smattering of pine and oak trees
dot the landscape. The grass is manicured and scythed short here, and the grey
walls of Fornost loom large to the southeast. A nondescript path leads through
the field, to the west and south.
name: Grassy Field near Fornost

desc:
Passing through the gate a sheltered garden is hidden away from the world here.
All around the scent of delicate flowers and herbs are noticed, with a peaceful
sound of gently running water. At a glance the garden is lovingly cared for in
an effort to create a quiet, peaceful place to rest.
name: Tranquil Garden

desc:
The stable is neat and orderly, with a large gate leading into the city on the
southern wall. The gate is made from thick cedar that was painted white besides
for the black posts that were used as a cross-brace for support. Two stalls,
both equally large stand side by side along the western wall of the stable. The
stalls were simply made, with one black post set in front of them where they
connect that rises to the ceiling; two small lanterns hang from the black post,
one on each side to provide each stall in the stable a dim light. A simple door
leading beyond the stable is in the north-east corner.
name: Miriath's Stable

desc:
The walls were formed from solid stone and painted dark grey with a white crown
moulding that has a beautiful marquetry design along the ceiling. Silver stones
in the corners of the moulding were embedded and shimmer softly, creating a very
relaxing ambience to the home. A large mirror with a black, hand-crafted frame
hangs on the eastern wall and two shiny and black sconces are on the wall with a
door between them to the south. A soft black carpet covers the marbled white
floor in the centre of the room and a bed in a black metal frame stands against
the northern wall. Along the western wall is a tall table with two chairs; the
chairs are silver and glossy, with black cushions covering them, contrasting
from the sheer and silver fabric that covers the entranceway next to the dining
area.
name: White-Stoned House

desc:
Silver railings trace the terrace on three sides and are covered in slender
strands of green ivy, with periwinkle blooms in the shape of tiny bells. Four
diamond-shaped pillars rise in each corner of the terrace, black and made of
stone, each with a silver basin at the top. A brilliant white flame rises from
each basin, providing a light and some warmth throughout the terrace. A little
hammock is suspended from the eve of the roof to the east where the terrace
railings connect to a stone building. A beautiful mural of a large and daunting
mountain range was painted on the wall behind the swaying hammock, with each
star painted above shimmering from some type of magic. A silver fabric was used
to cover the entranceway to the east.
name: Flowery Terrace

desc:
You stand under a big red sign which proclaims that you are at the entrance to
the well-known inn called 'The Crown of Arnor', or as people say around these
parts, just 'The Crown'. Some oxcarts have been parked here, and presumably
their drivers are inside enjoying a nice, hot meal.
name: Market Square
